student_verification.name: student
student_verification.declaration: I used Claude to help choose the dataset and write the preprocessing code. I ran the notebook myself, caught that the stats were showing zeros, debugged it with Claude, and confirmed the fix worked. The dataset justification and limitation analysis reflect my own understanding.

interactions.prompt: Question 2 reproduction on a toy dataset...so can you help me what should i do here for task 2.1 dataset selection and setup
interactions.response_summary: Suggested NLTK twitter_samples as the best dataset choice and explained why it fits the paper's method better than alternatives like IMDb or 20newsgroups.
interactions.student_modification: Ran the notebook and noticed the stats showed zeros for URLs, targets, and emoticons. Reported the issue back to Claude for debugging.

interactions.prompt: Total tokens: 72524 Stop words: 16710 (23.0%) URLs (||U||): 0 Targets (||T||): 0 Positive emoticons: 0 Negative emoticons: 0 — isn't this dataset wrong for the purpose?
interactions.response_summary: Diagnosed that the issue was a regex escaping bug (r'' prefix lost in JSON serialisation) not the dataset itself. Fixed the emoticon patterns and URL regex.
interactions.student_modification: Re-ran notebook after fix to verify stats were now non-zero.

interactions.prompt: still got this only [same zero stats]
interactions.response_summary: Found the second bug — TweetTokenizer splits ||P|| on | characters making .count('||P||') return 0. Fixed by switching to .split() for token counting.
interactions.student_modification: Verified fix resolved the zero counts.

interactions.prompt: can you keep things cleaner and like in bullets so as to easily understandable — Both markdown and code comments
interactions.response_summary: Rewrote all markdown explanation cells to start with 'What this does:' followed by tight bullets, and trimmed code comments to one short line per block.
